payload.title: household board as a seed
payload.text: household's kanban domain (capture/move/resolve over memory items, grouped into lanes) can run as a self seed — three commands + a board projector compiled from declarations — with zero kernel changes, driven by plain HTML forms AND by the brain calling the commands as tools. Tests the accessible-entry-point anchor and the household-to-self port. Comparison: household implements this as hand-written Go (event types, aggregate Apply, command service, projection package) requiring a rebuild to extend.

payload.name: capture
payload.description: Capture a household memory item onto the board. Join argv as the title; assign a sequential id (item-N counting existing memory.captured events); start it in the inbox lane.

payload.name: move
payload.description: Move an item to a lane. The argument is '<item ref> <stage>': the LAST word is the target lane (inbox, this_week, waiting, or done), and the rest identifies the item by id or by a case-insensitive title substring. Emit item.stage_changed.

payload.name: resolve
payload.description: Resolve (complete) an item, sending it to the done lane. The argument identifies the item by id or by a case-insensitive title substring. Emit item.resolved.

payload.name: board
payload.description: A household kanban board. Fold memory.captured, item.stage_changed, and item.resolved into items grouped by lane (inbox, this_week, waiting, done); item.resolved sends an item to done; item.stage_changed matches by id or title substring. Render a capture form at the top, then each lane as a section listing its item cards; each card shows the title and a move form (a select of the four lanes + a move button) and a resolve button, all plain forms posting to /run. Bare semantic HTML using the card/row/muted/tag classes.

payload.title: School form needs both signatures by Friday
